summary: Decompiled a large chunk of Kirby fighter ftKb logic, mainly SpecialLw/stone-related routines and several helper routines previously left as /// placeholders. The PR added concrete motion-var layout for Kirby SpecialLw, updated ftKb_Init.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed Fighter_GObj signatures, and introduced required includes for effects, fall-special/common fall handling, vector math, map collision helpers, and math functions. Evidence is limited to the PR dump and diff excerpt; there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added implementations in ftKb_Init.c for previously undecompiled Kirby routines including ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3570, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F36DC, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3B28, ftKb_SpecialLw_Enter, ftKb_SpecialAirLw_Enter, ftKb_SpecialLw1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialLw_Anim, ftKb_SpecialLwEnd_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirLw_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Anim, and ftKb_SpecialLw_IASA.
Added inline helpers ftKirbyDmgInline2, ftKbUnkInline, and fbKb_SpecialLw_IASA_Inline to share callback setup, repeated animation update logic, and stone-exit timing/input checks.

Added Kirby SpecialLw motion variables to union ftKb_MotionVars in types.h as struct ftKb_SpecialLWVars, covering timers, state fields, Vec3 history/current vectors, and a float angle/weight array at x88.
Changed ftKb_Init.h prototypes for ftKb_SpecialHi_800F36DC, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC, and ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3B28 from unknown return/params to void functions taking Fighter_GObj*.

Added includes for ef/efsync.h, ftCo_FallSpecial.h, lb/lbvector.h, mp/mplib.h, and MSL/math.h to support effect spawning, fall/freefall transitions, vector normalization, map-line queries, atan2f, and ABS/math usage.

SpecialLw enter routines initialize stone timers and state fields from ftKb_DatAttrs, reset cmd_vars, zero damage-related state, preserve x221C_b4 across Fighter_ChangeMotionState, install Kirby damage/death callbacks, start animation, and spawn effect IDs 0x49B/0x49C.
SpecialLw animation and IASA logic transitions between start/loop/end states using motion IDs 0x189, 0x18A, 0x18B, 0x18C, and 0x18D, with flags such as 0x04440012U and frame-remaining checks.

SpecialLw sliding helpers compute floor/slant vectors, normalize accumulated vector history, clamp slant angle and ground velocity by dat_attrs values, and update ground acceleration, animation velocity, and self velocity.
smart_moves: Introduced a dedicated SpecialLw motion-var struct before using fields extensively, making the decompiled routines readable enough to express vector history and timers instead of raw offsets.
Kept unknown/offset-based field names such as x0, x88, x221C_b4, and mv.co.common.x58 when semantic certainty was not shown in the evidence, avoiding over-naming speculative data.
Used small inline helpers to preserve likely matching-sensitive code shape while eliminating duplicated callback and animation update sequences.
Preserved x221C_b4 around Fighter_ChangeMotionState in multiple routines, suggesting attention to side effects from motion changes and matching behavior.

Added narrowly necessary includes instead of relying on placeholder declarations: efSync_Spawn, ftCo_80096900/ftCo_Fall_Enter, lbVector_Normalize, mpLib_800524DC, and atan2f all correspond to newly decompiled logic.
Header prototypes were updated only for functions whose signatures became known, improving type safety without broader speculative header churn.
decomp_lessons: For fighter move decomp, map the motion-var union first when a group of routines shares a packed per-action state block; this lets subsequent code use structured accesses and exposes repeated history-buffer patterns.

When a routine still has unclear semantics, stable address-based names such as ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC are acceptable, but parameter and return types should be tightened as soon as evidence supports them.

Kirby SpecialLw/stone logic depends on both fighter data attributes and persistent motion variables: timers, min/max stone time, slant thresholds, slide acceleration, slide max speed, and freefall toggle all appear in the decompiled routines.

Grounded movement routines often combine map collision normals, mpLib queries, vector normalization, friction multipliers, ground velocity clamps, animation velocity, and self velocity; future matching work should preserve these calculations rather than simplifying them too early.
Callback setup is part of move initialization matching: this PR shows take_dmg_cb and death2_cb being assigned during Kirby SpecialLw enter/end transitions.
Effect spawning may require saving the fighter part joint before motion changes; this PR uses FtPart_YRotN joint with efSync_Spawn for SpecialLw stone effects.
naming_conventions: Address-suffixed function names remain in use for not-yet-semantically-named helpers, e.g. ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3570 and ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3B28.
Typed fighter callbacks consistently take Fighter_GObj* gobj and retrieve Fighter* fp with GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Kirby-specific motion variables live under fp->mv.kb.specialhi and fp->mv.kb.speciallw.
Data attributes are accessed through ftKb_DatAttrs* dat_attr = fp->dat_attrs.
Uncertain fields keep offset-style names such as x18, x24, x84, x88, xC4, and x221C_b4 rather than invented semantic names.
Header comments preserve function address markers such as /* 0F36DC */ beside prototypes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK in SpecialLw enter and IASA routines, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching or to preserve expected frame layout.
Used inline helpers rather than standalone non-inline functions for repeated code patterns, likely to preserve compiler-generated layout in multiple callers.
Used explicit temporary variables such as temp_r28, temp_r30, temp_f1, temp_f2, var_f31, var_r4, and var_r5, matching common decomp style for register/stack-corresponding temporaries.
Preserved exact float constants and comparisons, including 0.017453292f, 0.25f, 0.9f, 1.0f, and 0.0f, which are important for matching floating-point codegen.
Used explicit clamps for ground velocity and ground acceleration against dat_attr->speciallw_slide_max_speed rather than abstract helper functions.
Used direct array indexing into x88[0] through x88[8] to model the observed motion-var layout and history/weight calculations.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: "Some old ftKb work I forgot to commit", so intent beyond committing old Kirby ftKb decomp work is weakly evidenced.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3570, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F36DC, ftKb_SpecialHi_800F37EC, or ftKb_SpecialHi_800F3B28 to semantic SpecialLw helper names.
Search for cleanup or review follow-ups around formatting in ftKb_Init.c, because the excerpt contains uneven spacing but no review comments in this slice.
Compare Kirby SpecialLw stone logic with other fighters or copied abilities to confirm meanings for ftKb_SpecialLWVars fields x0 through x88.
Search for future PRs that rename x221C_b4, mv.co.common.x58, mv.co.common.x4C, or SpecialLw vector history fields after stronger semantic evidence appears.
Check later matching status for ftKb_SpecialLw_IASA and the SpecialLw animation routines to see whether the inline helper shapes were retained.
